title: Daemon REST exposes complete agent memory operations
summary: After comparing AgentMemory's HTTP agent story, Kage daemon REST now exposes the same repo local memory primitives HTTP only agents need: /kage/context for combined recall, graph facts, validation, risk, and dependency c
body:
After comparing AgentMemory's HTTP-agent story, Kage daemon REST now exposes the same repo-local memory primitives HTTP-only agents need: /kage/context for combined recall, graph facts, validation, risk, and dependency context; /kage/capture and /kage/learn for durable packet writes; /kage/feedback for helpful/wrong/stale signals; plus /kage/setup-doctor for setup readiness. These endpoints reuse Kage's existing packets, graphs, recall, audit, and validation instead of creating a separate REST memory store.
Evidence: AgentMemory advertises broad REST support for non-MCP agents. Kage previously documented Aider REST mode with only recall, observe, and distill. The daemon now advertises and routes context, capture, learn, feedback, setup-doctor, recall, observe, distill, and reports.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp -- --test-name-pattern 'daemon doctor advertises complete REST memory operations|daemon context report gives REST agents combined memory graph and risk context|setup generates all-agent MCP configuration'; curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8891/kage/context -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"query":"setup hook readiness in viewer","limit":2}'; curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8891/kage/setup-doctor; git diff --check
